assertion_results.text: Response produces a rule { } block with proper YARA-L structure
Rule includes meta: section with at least author and description
events: filters USER_LOGIN and security_result.action = FAIL
assertion_results.evidence: Uses metadata.fail_reason != null instead of security_result.action = "FAIL"; uses == operator instead of =; includes hallucinated field metadata.log_source == "AUTH"

assertion_results.text: events: uses placeholder variable for user
assertion_results.evidence: Uses $login_failed as an event variable, not a placeholder variable for join key; grouping condition `where $login_failed.user_name == $login_failed.user_name` is tautological and wrong

assertion_results.text: match: groups by user variable over 10m
assertion_results.evidence: Uses `with max_span = 10m` which is invalid YARA-L syntax; grouping logic is malformed
#$login_failed >= 5 is wrong syntax — # prefix should not include $ for the variable name

assertion_results.text: Rule does NOT use regex on metadata.event_type
assertion_results.evidence: No regex on event_type — uses == comparison (though == itself is wrong operator)
